text.title: Vercel Deployment — Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules for Vercel deployments at solo SaaS scale.

rule.statement: Environment variables MUST be scoped to one of {Development, Preview, Production} via Vercel's env-scope selector — NEVER "All Environments". Secrets in Preview must NOT match Production secrets (separate DB credentials, separate API keys).

rationale.source: Vercel docs / 12-factor config + multiple real incidents 2022-2024

rule.rationale: "All Environments" is the leading cause of staging-data overwriting production. Different secrets per environment limit blast radius when a PR build runs against the wrong DB.

rule.statement: Before declaring `export const runtime = 'edge'` in a route, the file MUST be validated against the Edge-runtime allow-list (`templates/runtime-allow-list.yaml`): no Node-only APIs (`fs`, `child_process`, native crypto.timingSafeEqual prior to Edge crypto polyfill, `dns`). Failing files MUST stay on Node runtime.

rationale.source: Vercel Edge Functions runtime docs + community-reported regressions

rule.rationale: Edge-runtime adoption without checks produces 500s in production that local dev never sees. The allow-list pre-empts the class entirely.

rule.statement: ISR (`revalidate: N`) pages MUST include all tenant / user discriminators in their cache key (path params + searchParams used in fetch). Pages caching tenant-A's data and serving it to tenant-B is a data-leakage incident, not "cache misconfiguration".
rule.rationale: ISR caches by path. Pages reading user-context from cookies but caching by path serve the wrong user's data. The cache-key audit prevents the leak.

rule.statement: Rollback procedure MUST be: (a) identify last-known-good deployment ID, (b) run `vercel rollback <deployment_id>` (Pro plan) OR Vercel UI promote-previous (Hobby), (c) verify production traffic hits the rolled-back deployment via `/_vercel/info` check, (d) post incident log. Total elapsed time ≤ 5 minutes for a solo on-call.

rationale.source: Vercel rollback docs + SRE error-budget conventions

rule.rationale: A rollback that takes 30 minutes is a rebuild, not a rollback. The 5-minute target keeps the option viable; the verification step prevents "rolled back the wrong project".

rule.statement: Secrets used only at build-time (analytics keys, sentry DSN) are OK as env vars. Secrets used at runtime (DB passwords, third-party API keys) MUST be loaded from Vercel's encrypted env store AND NEVER reach client-side bundles. Build that exposes a `process.env.SECRET_*` to client code MUST fail CI.

rationale.source: Next.js / Vercel build-time env semantics + reported secret leaks

rule.rationale: `NEXT_PUBLIC_` prefix inadvertently exposed many production secrets when devs prefixed for convenience. CI guard is the only reliable boundary.

example.title: Safe Edge-runtime route
example.note: r2-compatible. Routes that only use Web APIs are safe for Edge.

example.title: Node-only API in Edge route (r2 violation)
example.note: r2 forces the developer to pick: drop Edge OR drop fs. The methodology refuses to ship this.
